context.summary: Turn your micro:bit into a simple compass which shows its bearing from magnetic North in degrees.
how_it_works: Your micro:bit has a built-in compass sensor called a magnetometer. You can use it to measure the Earthâs magnetic field and use it as a compass.
When you first use the micro:bit compass you have to calibrate it â a little game appears on the screen where you have to tilt the micro:bit to light up every LED, then youâre ready to go.

When you press the button A input, the micro:bit takes a reading from the compass sensor and shows the deviceâs numerical compass bearing on the LED display output. Point the micro:bit North and you should see a reading of 0 degrees.
improve_it: Add another button to recalibrate the compass.
Have the micro:bit make a sound when itâs pointing in a particular direction â this could be useful to help navigation when you canât look at a display or for people with impaired vision.
Make the micro:bit display letters or arrows to show if itâs pointing North, South, East or West.
